config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan hak individu adalah dasar agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kalau prioritas masyarakat selalu diletakkan di atas individu, saya khawatir itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society’s interests should matter, but I do not think they should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, people often understand that public order, collective stability, and long-term development are important, yet I still believe individual rights need clear limits that the state cannot cross. If “society first” becomes a blank check, it can easily excuse unfair treatment, and that is dangerous no matter how noble the goal sounds. The better principle is balance: protect the common good, but only through rules that still respect basic individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membenarkan penyalahgunaan wewenang, atau mengorbankan orang kecil yang tidak punya daya tawar. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak individu yang jelas, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah berubah jadi alat pembenaran ketidakadilan. Jadi, bagi saya yang utama bukan memilih salah satu secara mutlak, melainkan membatasi kepentingan kolektif agar tidak melanggar hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society’s interests should not automatically take priority over individual rights, even if I understand why many people in China value collective stability and long-term public goals. In my view, when “the common good” is treated as a higher principle by default, it can too easily become a justification for silencing inconvenient people or ignoring basic fairness. A society is stronger when it protects individual rights firmly and then pursues collective goals within those limits, not the other way around. I would only accept overriding individual rights in very narrow, clearly defined emergencies, with strict legal checks.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil, membungkam kritik, atau menutupi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, jadi saya tidak percaya itu bisa dijadikan prinsip utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih kuat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar yang tegas dan batas hukum yang jelas. Kalau hak individu bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa perlu, itu bukan keadilan sosial, melainkan pintu menuju ketidakadilan yang dibenarkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should routinely take priority over the individual, because in my view that idea is too easy to abuse. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people value social stability, public order, and collective goals, but I have also seen how quickly “for the greater good” can become a way to silence criticism or ignore fairness. A society is not truly strong if it asks individuals to sacrifice basic rights whenever the majority or the state says so. The better principle is that collective interests matter, but only within firm legal limits that protect each person’s basic dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
